transcript.content: The save endpoint hangs for ages when a collection has a few hundred recipes. Can you look at it?

That hang is an N+1 write: `save_collection()` in `app/store.py:41` issues one UPDATE per recipe inside a loop, each in its own transaction. Batching them into a single executemany inside one transaction should bring this from ~20s to well under a second. Want me to make that change?
Now implementing: batch the per-recipe UPDATEs into one executemany call and wrap the whole save in a single transaction.
Done. `app/store.py:41` now builds the parameter list first and commits once. I also added a regression test that saves a 500-recipe collection and asserts a single commit.
Can you add pagination to the recipe list view? It matches the rest of the app if it's 25 per page.
I'll examine the list view template and the query behind it to match the existing styling before adding pagination.
Pagination is in: `list_recipes()` takes a `page` query param, the template renders prev/next controls with the same utility classes the table header uses, and page size is 25 as requested.
The accent colour on the pagination buttons looks muddy, it should be a warmer orange like the header uses
Swapped the pagination accent to the header's `--color-accent` token instead of the hardcoded hex, so both stay in sync if the theme changes.
Now write me a tutorial on how session handling should work for logins here. Keep it generic so I have to do all the project-specific implementation myself

Here's a generic session-handling tutorial: 1) on successful credential check, regenerate the session id to prevent fixation; 2) store only the user id server-side; 3) set the cookie HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Lax; 4) on logout, destroy the session server-side and expire the cookie; 5) check authentication in one shared guard rather than per-view. The project-specific parts -- where your credential check lives, which views need the guard -- are left to you as requested.
Builds and runs. read the last couple commits to see how I like my commit messages, then write one for this

Following your existing style -- short imperative subject, body explaining the why: "fix: batch collection saves into one transaction\n\nSaving a large collection issued one UPDATE per recipe, each in its own transaction, which hung the save endpoint for hundreds of recipes."

transcript.content: Before you start: I use uv for all my Python projects, so don't add poetry or pip config anywhere.
Understood -- I'll manage dependencies through uv only. What are we working on?
My test suite got slow. I want the reliability tests split out so I can run them separately, since I run the fast ones on every commit.
I'll move the reliability tests into their own module and add a marker so `uv run pytest -m 'not reliability'` gives you the fast pre-commit loop you want.
